title: urceola torulosa
intro: Urceola torulosa, also known as the 'Balsam of the Philippines', is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Urceola torulosa include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in wound healing and skin conditions like eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional folk medicine.
